items.fact: AMPK acts as a cellular energy sensor that activates catabolic pathways and inhibits anabolic pathways during low-energy states, such as fasting or exercise.
items.mechanism: Activated by rising AMP:ATP ratio; phosphorylates key enzymes to stimulate glucose uptake, fatty acid oxidation, and mitochondrial biogenesis while suppressing lipogenesis and protein synthesis.
items.population: General population, especially athletes and individuals with metabolic disorders
items.source_context: chapter 7  Integration and Regulation of Metabolism and the Impact of Exercise and Sport 249...Amp-Activated Protein Kinase  252

items.fact: Leucine, isoleucine, and valine are primarily catabolized in skeletal muscle—not the liver—and serve as important energy substrates during fasting and exercise.
items.mechanism: Undergo transamination in muscle; carbon skeletons enter TCA cycle via acyl-CoA dehydrogenases; leucine stimulates mTORC1 signaling for protein synthesis.
items.population: Athletes, elderly, critically ill patients
items.source_context: Hepatic Catabolism and Uses of Branched-Chain Amino Acids  203

items.fact: Glutamine is the most abundant free amino acid in human plasma and serves as a major fuel for enterocytes, lymphocytes, and renal tubular cells.
items.mechanism: Metabolized to glutamate and ammonia in kidneys; supports acid-base balance via ammoniagenesis; maintains intestinal barrier function and immune cell proliferation.
items.population: Critically ill, post-surgical, or malnourished individuals
items.source_context: Glutamine and the Muscle, Intestine, Liver, and Kidneys  226

items.fact: Alanine functions as a key nitrogen carrier from muscle to liver via the glucose-alanine cycle during fasting.
items.mechanism: Muscle releases alanine derived from pyruvate and branched-chain amino acid catabolism; liver converts it to glucose via gluconeogenesis.
items.population: Fasting or calorie-restricted individuals
items.source_context: Alanine and the Liver and Muscle  228

items.fact: Folate deficiency causes megaloblastic macrocytic anemia due to impaired DNA synthesis and red blood cell maturation.
items.mechanism: Required for one-carbon transfers in nucleotide synthesis (e.g., thymidylate formation); deficiency leads to uracil misincorporation and DNA strand breaks.
items.population: Pregnant women, alcohol-dependent individuals, elderly with poor diet
items.source_context: Folate  344...Deficiency: Megaloblastic Macrocytic Anemia  351

items.fact: Vitamin B12 deficiency also results in megaloblastic anemia but is distinguished by concurrent neurological damage (e.g., peripheral neuropathy, subacute combined degeneration).
items.mechanism: Essential cofactor for methionine synthase (affecting folate cycling) and methylmalonyl-CoA mutase; deficiency causes homocysteine accumulation and odd-chain fatty acid metabolism defects.
items.population: Older adults, vegans, individuals with pernicious anemia or gastric atrophy
items.source_context: Vitamin B12 (Cobalamin)  354...Deficiency: Megaloblastic Macrocytic Anemia  358

items.fact: Pantothenic acid is a component of coenzyme A (CoA) and acyl carrier protein (ACP), essential for acetyl group transfer and fatty acid synthesis.
items.mechanism: Forms the reactive thiol end of CoA, enabling activation of carboxylic acids (e.g., acetate → acetyl-CoA) for entry into TCA cycle or lipid biosynthesis.
items.population: General population; deficiency rare but seen in severe malnutrition
items.source_context: Pantothenic Acid  334...Functions and Mechanisms of Action  336

items.fact: Biotin serves as a covalently bound cofactor for carboxylase enzymes involved in gluconeogenesis, fatty acid synthesis, and amino acid catabolism.
items.mechanism: Carries activated CO₂ in reactions catalyzed by acetyl-CoA carboxylase, propionyl-CoA carboxylase, and methylcrotonyl-CoA carboxylase.
items.population: Pregnant women (increased demand), individuals consuming raw egg whites chronically
items.source_context: Biotin  338...Functions and Mechanisms of Action  339

items.fact: Vitamin D deficiency causes rickets in children and osteomalacia in adults due to impaired bone mineralization.
items.mechanism: Vitamin D promotes intestinal calcium and phosphorus absorption and regulates bone remodeling via osteoblast/osteoclast activity.
items.population: Children, older adults, individuals with limited sun exposure
items.source_context:
Vitamin D  390
Deficiency: Rickets and Osteomalacia  399

items.fact: Calcium is essential for bone and teeth structure, muscle contraction, nerve transmission, and blood clotting.
items.mechanism: Acts as a cofactor in enzymatic reactions and signal transduction; stored in hydroxyapatite crystals in bone matrix.
items.population: All ages, especially adolescents and postmenopausal women
items.source_context:
Calcium  425
Functions and Mechanisms of Action  431

items.fact: Phosphorus works synergistically with calcium in bone mineralization and is a key component of ATP, DNA, and cell membranes.
items.mechanism: Forms phosphate esters critical for energy metabolism and genetic material synthesis; buffers intracellular pH.
items.population: General population; excess intake may impair calcium balance
items.source_context:
Phosphorus  438
Functions and Mechanisms of Action  440

items.fact: Magnesium serves as a cofactor for over 300 enzymatic reactions, including those involved in energy production and protein synthesis.
items.mechanism: Stabilizes ATP structure and modulates ion channels (e.g., calcium and potassium) in excitable tissues.
items.population: Individuals with gastrointestinal disorders, type 2 diabetes, or chronic alcohol use
items.source_context:
Magnesium  443
Functions and Mechanisms of Action  445

items.fact: Sodium is the primary extracellular cation and critical for maintaining fluid balance, nerve conduction, and muscle function.
items.mechanism: Regulated by renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS) and vasopressin to control osmotic pressure and blood volume.
items.population: General population; excessive intake linked to hypertension
items.source_context:
Sodium  462
Absorption, Transport, and Function  463
Renin-Angiotensin-Aldosterone System  459

items.fact: Potassium is the major intracellular cation and counterbalances sodium to maintain cellular membrane potential and heart rhythm.
items.mechanism: Facilitates Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase pump activity; influences vascular smooth muscle tone and insulin secretion.
items.population: Hypertensive individuals, those on diuretics, or with chronic kidney disease
items.source_context:
Potassium  466
Absorption, Transport, and Function  466
Deficiency and Toxicity  467

items.fact: Chloride is the main extracellular anion, essential for gastric acid production, fluid balance, and acid-base regulation.
items.mechanism: Cotransported with sodium; component of hydrochloric acid (HCl) in stomach and part of bicarbonate buffer system.
items.population: General population; deficiency often accompanies sodium loss (e.g., vomiting, diarrhea)
items.source_context:
Chloride  467
Absorption, Transport, and Secretion  468
Functions  468

items.fact: Iodine is required for synthesis of thyroid hormones T3 and T4, which regulate basal metabolic rate and development.
items.mechanism: Incorporated into tyrosine residues of thyroglobulin in the thyroid gland to form thyroid hormones.
items.population: Pregnant women, infants, and populations in iodine-deficient regions
items.source_context:
Iodine  530
Functions and Mechanisms of Action  531
Deficiency  534

items.fact: Chromium enhances insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake by potentiating insulin receptor signaling.
items.mechanism: Forms part of chromodulin, a low-molecular-weight oligopeptide that amplifies insulin receptor kinase activity.
items.population: Individuals with insulin resistance or type 2 diabetes
items.source_context:
Chromium  527
Functions and Mechanisms of Action  528

items.fact: Manganese is a cofactor for enzymes involved in bone formation, amino acid metabolism, and antioxidant defense (e.g., Mn-SOD).
items.mechanism: Activates glycosyltransferases for proteoglycan synthesis and superoxide dismutase for mitochondrial ROS scavenging.
items.population: Infants fed soy-based formulas (higher Mn), individuals with liver disease
items.source_context:
Manganese  536
Functions and Mechanisms of Action  536
Deficiency  538

items.fact: 最新膳食摄入推荐已更新，并扩展了其在肌肉功能中的作用
items.mechanism: 调节肌肉细胞钙信号及基因表达，影响肌纤维合成与功能
items.population: 一般人群，尤其关注肌肉健康者
items.source_context: Chapter 10  Fat-Soluble Vitamins: 'expanded the discussion of the roles of vitamin E in gene expression and vitamin D in muscle function'；'included the latest dietary intake recommendations for vitamin D'

items.fact: 与骨骼健康密切相关
items.mechanism: 作为γ-谷氨酰羧化酶的辅因子，促进骨钙素等骨基质蛋白的活化
items.population: 中老年人、骨质疏松风险人群
items.source_context: Chapter 10: 'updated and expanded the coverage of the relationships between the fat-soluble vitamins and diseases including... vitamin K and bone health'

items.fact: 最新膳食摄入推荐已更新，吸收机制及骨形成/骨吸收过程得到扩展
items.mechanism: 跨膜转运（TRPV6、CaBP-D9k）、激素调控（PTH、维生素D）、成骨/破骨细胞平衡
items.population: 各年龄段，尤以儿童、孕妇、绝经后女性为重点
items.source_context: Chapter 11  Major Minerals: 'included the latest dietary intake recommendations for calcium'；'expanded the discussion of calcium absorption as well as bone formation and resorption'

items.fact: 三者与高血压存在关联
items.mechanism: 钠升高血管张力与血容量；钙/镁拮抗钠致血管收缩，调节平滑肌细胞内钙浓度
items.population: 高血压前期及高血压患者
items.source_context: Chapter 11: 'updated the discussion of the associations between sodium, calcium, and magnesium and hypertension in the Perspective'

items.fact: 与感冒、癌症、心血管病及眼健康相关
items.mechanism: 抗氧化、胶原合成、内皮功能维护、铁吸收促进及基因表达调控
items.population: 免疫力低下者、吸烟者、慢性病高风险人群
items.source_context: Chapter 9  Water-Soluble Vitamins: 'updated and expanded coverage of the relationships between some of the water-soluble vitamins and various diseases including, for example, vitamin C and colds, cancer, heart disease, and eye health'

items.fact: 与痴呆、心血管病、癌症及神经管缺陷相关
items.mechanism: 参与一碳代谢，影响DNA甲基化与合成，缺乏致同型半胱氨酸升高及神经发育异常
items.population: 育龄妇女、老年人、高同型半胱氨酸血症者
items.source_context: Chapter 9: 'folate and dementia, heart disease, cancer, and neural tube defects'

items.fact: 与心血管病及神经管缺陷相关
items.mechanism: 协同叶酸参与同型半胱氨酸再甲基化，缺乏导致甲基丙二酸蓄积及神经髓鞘损伤
items.population: 老年人、素食者、胃肠道吸收障碍者
items.source_context: Chapter 9: 'vitamin B12 and heart disease and neural tube defects'

items.fact: 新增其在体内的多种生理角色
items.mechanism: 构成ATP、磷脂、核酸及羟基磷灰石；参与信号转导（磷酸化）和pH缓冲
items.population: 一般人群，肾功能不全者需限制
items.source_context: Chapter 11: 'added coverage of the roles of phosphorus in the body'

items.fact: 调控水与钠重吸收及钾排泄
items.mechanism: 通过V2受体激活集合管AQP2水通道；间接影响钠钾转运
items.population: 脱水、低血容量或SIADH患者
items.source_context: Chapter 12  Water and Electrolytes: 'expanded the discussion of the role of vasopressin (ADH) in the reabsorption of water and sodium and the excretion of potassium'

items.nutrient: 肾素-血管紧张素II-醛固酮系统（RAAS）
items.fact: 协同调控水钠重吸收与钾排泄
items.mechanism: 血管紧张素II刺激近曲小管Na⁺重吸收；醛固酮促进远曲小管Na⁺重吸收/K⁺排泄
items.population: 心衰、肝硬化、肾病综合征等体液失衡患者
items.source_context: Chapter 12: 'expanded coverage of the role of the renin-angiotensin II-aldosterone system in the reabsorption of water and sodium and the excretion of potassium'
